task_completion.description: Create exterior perimeter walls forming a rectangle approximately 8 meters long by 4 meters wide.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A rectangular wall outline visible in the 2D plan view. The proportions should clearly reflect a 2:1 ratio (8x4), verifiable via the background grid or dimension lines.

task_completion.description: Incorporate at least two interior partition walls, one entry door, one interior door, and at least three windows.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 2D plan or 3D view shows interior dividing walls, an exterior door on the perimeter, an interior door connecting rooms, and at least three window objects placed on the walls.

task_completion.description: Define and name four functional zones: living area, kitchen/dining area, bedroom, and a fully enclosed bathroom.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text labels in the 2D plan view showing the names of the four required rooms (e.g., 'Living Room', 'Kitchen', 'Bedroom', 'Bathroom'). The bathroom area must be fully enclosed by walls.

task_completion.description: Furnish every zone with appropriate items from the catalog.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Furniture and fixtures (e.g., bed, sofa, kitchen cabinets, toilet/shower) are visible in the 2D plan or 3D view across all four defined zones.

task_completion.description: Add at least two dimension lines documenting the overall length and width.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Explicit dimension lines with measurements (lines with arrows at the ends and numeric values) drawn alongside the exterior walls in the 2D plan view.
The Sweet Home 3D window title bar shows 'tiny_house_plan.sh3d' or a save dialog confirms the file is being saved in the Documents/SweetHome3D directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the Sweet Home 3D graphical interface to design the floor plan.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Sweet Home 3D application open with active interactions (mouse cursor, selected tools, dragging items from the catalog), rather than just terminal commands.

integrity.description: The floor plan was built from scratch rather than loaded from an existing template.
integrity.visual_evidence: Trajectory screenshots show a progression from a blank canvas to partial walls, and finally to a fully furnished house.

integrity.description: The agent saved the file using the application's save functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Save' or 'Save As' dialog box is visible, or the application title bar updates to the target filename.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path is /home/ga/Documents/SweetHome3D/tiny_house_plan.sh3d. The house must be approximately 8x4 meters. It must have exactly four named rooms (Living Room, Kitchen, Bedroom, Bathroom), at least 2 interior walls, 1 entry door, 1 interior door, and 3 windows. All zones must be furnished, and at least two dimension lines must be present. The maximum score is 100, and the pass threshold is 60.
